identity.mission: Transform email from an infinite inbox into a decision panel. Reduce cognitive load. Prioritize clarity and calm.
identity.tone: Premium, Calm, Focused, Deep Space, Intelligent
identity.style: Dark Glassmorphism, Neural Network Aesthetics, Minimalist

theme.background_style: Neural Network Image with Heavy Dark Overlay

rules: NO REDS allowed except for critical errors (and even then, keep it muted if possible).
Cyan usage must be strictly limited to 1-3% of the screen (active states, halos, key indicators).
Backgrounds must never be pure black (#000000) unless it's a specific deep void effect.
Use 'Tinted Greys' (Slate) to maintain the blue undertone.

overview.rules: Max 3 priority items. 'Silence Mode' toggle prominent.

messages.rules: Card-IA must be context-aware, floating or docked to the right of the reading pane.

Card-IA.description: The AI Assistant for Email. Not a chat. A proactive helper.
Card-IA.style: Glassmorphism Premium + Blue Halo. Distinct from other cards.

PriorityItem.description: High-level summary card for the top 3 emails.
PriorityItem.style: Bento Grid Item. High contrast. Clear action.

Navigation.style: Vertical Sidebar (collapsed on mobile) or Top Bar (minimal). Glass effect.

images.description: Abstract blue lines and dots on black background. Use as global background with overlay.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text is Slate-100 or Slate-200 on dark backgrounds.

setup: Install 'framer-motion' for smooth transitions (if not present).
Configure Tailwind for 'class' based dark mode (already set).
implementation_steps: 1. Set up the global layout with the Neural Network background and dark overlay.
2. Build the 'Glass' utility classes in Tailwind layer components.
3. Create the 'Overview' page with the Bento Grid layout.
4. Implement the 'Messages' view with the Split layout.
5. Build the 'Card-IA' component with the specific Halo effect.
6. Ensure all text is legible against the glass backgrounds.
critical_constraints: DO NOT use Red unless absolutely necessary for a critical error.
DO NOT use generic 'Card' components without adding the custom glass/border styles.
Keep the 'Cyan' accent minimal (1-3%). It is for 'Life/Latency/Halo' effects only.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in '/app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx'
